data.title: epic: review and normalize repo-local skills/ after move from core
data.description:
## Context

`skills/` is now acting as the repo-local staging area for shared skills that need review. Current inventory:

* `skill-creator`
* `skogai-argc`
* `skogai-bats-testing`
* `skogai-docs`
* `skogai-git`
* `skogai-gita`
* `skogai-gptme`
* `skogai-hook-debugger`
* `skogai-jq`
* `skogai-mcp-builder`
* `skogai-planning-with-files`
* `skogai-prompting`
* `skogai-routing`
* `skogai-skill-creator`
* `skogai-skills`
* `skogai-todos`
* `skogai-workflow`
* `skogai-wrapup`

At the same time, the currently loaded core plugin only still contains `plugins/core/skills/skogai-routing/`.

## Why this needs an epic

The moved set has systemic review work that is better tracked by concern area than by opening one issue per skill:

* legacy `~/.claude/skills/` path assumptions and other stale runtime instructions
* overlapping/canonical-conflicting creator/router skills
* inconsistent [SKILL.md](<http://SKILL.md>) structure and naming
* external-tool and API claims that need freshness verification
* no automated validation for the shared `skills/` tree

## Outcome wanted

Use child issues to decide the canonical shared-skill set and make `skills/` safe to review incrementally in separate sessions.

## Done when

* current path/runtime assumptions are corrected
* canonical ownership between overlapping skills is decided
* structural conventions are applied consistently enough to be reviewable
* validation exists to prevent obvious regressions
* tool/API-facing skills have explicit freshness review coverage
